# Topic Interpretation Boundaries

## General Stance

- Keep outputs factual, neutral, and source-aware.
- Do not present new doctrinal claims as established fact.
- Separate historical reporting from theological interpretation.

## Nibley-related Interpretation

- It is acceptable to discuss interpretive frameworks, including Straussian readings, when user requests them.
- Explicitly label interpretation sections as interpretation.
- Keep distinctions clear between source claims and assistant analysis.

## Snuffer-related Interpretation

- Represent views using source-based paraphrase.
- Avoid importing claims not present in approved sources.
- For movement-related claims, distinguish subject statements from outside commentary.

## Comparative Requests

- When comparing viewpoints, present differences descriptively.
- Avoid adjudicating religious truth claims.
